_metadata.dataset_name: GST Accounts and Records Rules — Comprehensive
_metadata.source_authority: Central Board of Indirect Taxes and Customs (CBIC) + Ministry of Corporate Affairs (MCA)
governing_acts: Central Goods and Services Tax Act, 2017 — Sections 35 and 36
_metadata.last_amended: Companies (Accounts) Fourth Amendment Rules, 2022 (08.08.2022); Notification No. 20/2024-Central Tax (01.11.2024)

rules.title: Mandatory Books of Account — Registered Persons Under GST
rules.description: Every registered person shall keep and maintain, at his principal place of business, a true and correct account of the following.
mandatory_accounts: (b) Inward and outward supply of goods or services or both
(f) Such other particulars as may be prescribed (per Rule 56)
rules.multiple_places_of_business: Where a person has multiple places of business, accounts related to each place must be kept at that place
rules.electronic_records: Accounts may be maintained in electronic form as per Rule 57
rules.additional_requirements_by_commissioner: Commissioner may, by notification, prescribe additional accounts or documents to be maintained by a specified class of taxable persons (Section 35(3))
rules.relaxation_by_commissioner: Commissioner may relax the provisions for a class of taxable persons who are not in a position to maintain prescribed records (Section 35(4))
rules.penalty_for_non_compliance: Section 122 of CGST Act — up to Rs. 10,000 or amount of tax evaded, whichever is higher

rules.title: Accounts and Records by Warehouse/Godown Owners and Transporters
rules.description: Persons engaged in transporting goods or operating a warehouse/godown must also maintain prescribed accounts.
requirements: Transporters: must maintain a true and correct record of goods transported, delivered, and stored in transit — including GSTIN of registered consigner and consignee for each branch

Warehouse/godown operators: must maintain books of account with respect to the period for which goods remain in the warehouse — including particulars relating to dispatch, movement, receipt and disposal of goods
Persons acting as carrier or clearing and forwarding agent on behalf of a registered person: must maintain records in respect of goods handled and produce on demand by proper officer
Enrolment: Persons who are required to maintain records but are not registered must submit details in FORM GST ENR-01 on the GST portal (Rule 58)

rules.title: Period of Retention of Accounts and Records — GST
rules.description: Every registered person must retain books of account and other records for a minimum of 72 months (6 years) from the due date of filing the annual return for the year.
conditions: Minimum retention period: 72 months (6 years) from the due date of Annual Return (GSTR-9) for the relevant financial year
Example: Records for FY 2024-25 (Annual Return due 31 Dec 2025) to be kept until 31 December 2031

If a registered person is a party to an appeal, revision, or investigation: records must be retained for 1 year after final disposal of such proceedings OR for 72 months from due date of annual return — whichever is LATER
Retention applies to all documents including invoices, bills of supply, credit/debit notes, delivery challans, accounts, and vouchers
Electronic records must comply with Rule 57 requirements for backup and accessibility
Note: Companies Act 2013 Section 128(5) requires 8 years — the more stringent of the two laws (8 years) should be followed for companies

rules.title: Additional Records — Imports, Exports, RCM Supplies
rules.description: Every registered person must maintain, in addition to the accounts under Section 35(1), a true and correct account of goods/services imported, exported, or where tax is payable on reverse charge.

rules.title: Stock Accounts — Non-Composition Registered Persons
rules.description: Every registered person (other than a composition dealer) must maintain accounts of stock.
stock_account_contents: Goods lost, stolen, destroyed, written off
Goods disposed of by way of gift or free sample
Applicable to: raw materials, finished goods, scrap, and wastage

rules.title: Separate Account for Advances
rules.description: Every registered person must maintain a separate account of advances received, paid, and adjustments made.
key_conditions: Advances received for future supply of services must be tracked (GST payable on advances for services)
Advances paid to suppliers must be separately tracked for ITC purposes
Adjustments of advances against invoices must be documented and traceable
Unadjusted advance balances at year-end must be reconcilable with receipt/refund vouchers

rules.title: ITC and Output Tax Accounts
rules.description: Every registered person (other than composition dealer) must maintain accounts of ITC and output tax.
accounts_required: Input tax credit availed on each invoice — with document reference
Input tax credit utilised — broken down by IGST, CGST, SGST/UTGST, Cess
Output tax payable — broken down by IGST, CGST, SGST/UTGST, Cess
Output tax paid — by cash and by ITC utilisation
Input tax credit reversed — with reason and reference
Closing balance of ITC in Electronic Credit Ledger

rules.title: Invoice Register — Inward and Outward
rules.description: Every registered person must maintain a separate register for tax invoices, credit notes, debit notes, delivery challans issued and received.
outward_supply_register_contents: CGST, SGST, IGST, UTGST, Cess amount
inward_supply_register_contents: Value of supply and taxable value

rules.title: Supplier and Recipient Details to Be Maintained
rules.description: Every registered person must maintain details of parties from whom goods/services were received and to whom goods/services were supplied.
required_details: (a) Names and complete addresses of suppliers from whom taxable goods/services were received
(b) Names and complete addresses of persons to whom goods/services were supplied, where required under Chapter VII
(c) Complete address of premises where goods are stored by the registered person, including goods stored during transit, along with particulars of stock stored
rules.undeclared_premises_penalty: If taxable goods are found stored at premises not declared under this rule, without valid documents, the proper officer shall determine tax payable on such goods as if they were supplied by the registered person (Rule 56(6))

rules.title: Location of Books of Account
rules.description: Books of account must be maintained at the principal place of business AND at each additional place of business as mentioned in the GST registration certificate.
conditions: Manual books: must be kept at the physical place of business
Electronic records: must be accessible at every related place of business where they are maintained digitally
Books include any electronic form of data stored on any electronic device
If books are found at premises other than those on the registration certificate — presumed to belong to the registered person unless proved otherwise (Rule 56(10))

rules.title: No Erasure or Overwriting in Records; Edit Log for Electronic Records
rules.description: Entries in registers, accounts, and documents must not be erased, effaced, or overwritten. All corrections must be made by scoring out under attestation.
conditions: Manual records: incorrect entries (other than clerical errors) must be scored out (struck through) under attestation, and the correct entry must be recorded
Electronic records: a log of every entry edited or deleted must be maintained — equivalent to audit trail
No entry in any register or account shall be erased or made unreadable
Clerical corrections may be made without attestation, but corrections of substance require attestation
rules.cross_reference_companies_act: This mirrors the audit trail requirement under Rule 3(1) of Companies (Accounts) Rules 2014 (as amended): accounting software must have a feature for recording audit trail of every transaction, including date of each change, and the audit trail must not be disabled (effective 01-04-2021)

rules.title: Serial Numbering of Manual Books of Account
rules.description: Each volume of books of account maintained manually by a registered person must be serially numbered.
conditions: All volumes (cash book, purchase ledger, sales ledger, day book, etc.) must be serially numbered
Serial numbering ensures accountability and prevents insertion or removal of pages

rules.title: Monthly Production Accounts for Manufacturers
rules.description: Every registered person engaged in manufacturing goods must maintain monthly production accounts.
required_production_account_contents: Quantitative details of raw materials or services used in the manufacture of goods
Quantitative details of waste or by-products including the scrap and the value thereof

rules.title: Accounts Specific to Service Providers
rules.description: Every registered person providing services must maintain accounts showing quantitative details of goods used in provision of each service.
required_accounts: Quantitative details of goods used in provision of each service
Details of input services used in provision of each service
Total amount received for provision of each service — categorised by taxable, exempt, inter-State, intra-State

rules.title: Accounts for Agents
rules.description: Every agent must maintain accounts showing the details of authorisations received, goods received and supplied on behalf of each principal, and invoices issued by/on behalf of the agent.
required_agent_accounts: Details of authorisation received from each principal to receive/supply goods or services on their behalf
Details of goods or services received on behalf of each principal, with value
Details of goods or services supplied on behalf of each principal, with value
Details of accounts furnished to each principal
Tax paid (if any) in connection with the supply made on behalf of the principal
Invoices issued by the agent on behalf of the principal

rules.title: Electronic Records Authentication and Preservation Period
rules.description: Records maintained in electronic form must be authenticated by digital signature and preserved for the prescribed period.
conditions: Electronic records must be authenticated by means of a digital signature
All accounts, invoices, bills of supply, credit/debit notes, delivery challans must be preserved for the period specified in Section 36 (72 months from due date of annual return)
Manual records at related places of business; electronic records accessible at all related places
On demand by proper officer: electronic records must be produced in hard copy or electronically readable format (Rule 57(2))

rules.title: On-Demand Production of Books; Legal Books Must Also Be Maintained
rules.description: Every registered person must produce their books of account on demand by the proper officer, and must also maintain any books required under other applicable laws.
conditions: Rule 56(17): Persons handling goods on behalf of registered persons (carriers, clearing agents) must maintain true and correct records and produce on demand

Rule 56(18): Every registered person must, on demand, produce the books of accounts which he is required to maintain under any law for the time being in force (not just GST law) — includes Companies Act books, Income Tax books, etc.
Proper officer has the right to inspect books during investigation, audit, or assessment

rules.title: Generation and Maintenance of Electronic Records
rules.description: Specific technical requirements for registered persons who maintain books of account in electronic form.
requirements: (1) Proper electronic backup must be maintained and preserved so that records can be restored within a reasonable time if destroyed by accident or natural causes
(2) On demand, registered person must produce records or documents duly authenticated, in hard copy or electronically readable format

(3) Where accounts/records are stored electronically, the registered person must provide on demand: (a) details of such files; (b) passwords of such files; (c) explanation for codes used; (d) any other information required for access; and (e) a sample copy in print form of the information stored
Backup media: offline/cloud backup acceptable provided it is accessible and restorable
Format: records must remain in original format or a format that accurately represents the original

rules.title: Records to Be Maintained by Transporters and Warehouse Operators — Enrolment
rules.description: Owners/operators of warehouses, godowns, or storage places, and transporters who are not registered under GST but are required to maintain records, must enrol on the GST portal.
conditions: Persons required to maintain records under Section 35(2) but who are not registered: must submit details in FORM GST ENR-01 electronically on the GST Common Portal
A unique enrolment number is generated on submission and communicated to the person
Transporter registered in more than one State/UT: gets a common enrolment number applicable across all States
Transporters must maintain: records of goods transported, delivered, and stored in transit with GSTIN of consigner and consignee for each branch
Warehouse operators must maintain: period of goods storage, dispatch, movement, receipt and disposal particulars

rules.title: Companies Act: Accrual Basis + Double Entry — Mandatory
rules.description: Every company must prepare and maintain books of account on accrual basis using the double-entry system of accounting, giving a true and fair view of the state of affairs.
conditions: Accrual basis: revenues and expenses recognised when they occur, not when cash is received/paid
Double-entry system: every debit has a corresponding credit; trial balance must tally
Books must cover: transactions at the registered office AND all branch offices
Books may be kept at another location in India (not the registered office) after filing Form AOC-5 with ROC within 7 days of decision
If books are kept outside India in branches: summarised returns must be sent to registered office at quarterly intervals
rules.applicable_to: All companies under the Companies Act 2013, including foreign companies operating in India
rules.penalty: Liable persons (MD, WTD in charge of finance, CFO, or designated person) — fine not less than Rs. 50,000 up to Rs. 5,00,000

rules.title: Companies Act: 8-Year Retention of Books of Account
rules.description: Books of account, other books, papers, and vouchers relevant to entries in books must be kept in good order for not less than 8 financial years immediately preceding the current financial year.
conditions: Minimum 8 financial years of records must be retained
If company is less than 8 years old: retain since incorporation
If Central Government orders investigation under Chapter XIV: retain for such longer period as directed
Applies to physical and electronic records
Electronic records: backup must be kept on servers physically located in India
rules.reconciliation_with_gst: GST requires 72 months (6 years) from due date of annual return; Companies Act requires 8 years from current year. Companies should follow the more stringent requirement = 8 years minimum (for records common to both).

rules.title: Audit Trail in Accounting Software — Mandatory from 01-04-2021
rules.description: Companies maintaining books of account in electronic mode using accounting software must ensure the software has an audit trail feature that records every transaction with edit log, and this audit trail must not be disabled.
requirements: Accounting software must have a feature for recording an audit trail (edit log) for every transaction
Edit log must capture: date of each change, nature of change, user making the change
Audit trail must be maintained for the entire financial year
Audit trail cannot be disabled at any time — enabling/disabling is itself a recordable event
Cloud-based software: provider must ensure audit trail feature is always active
Back-up of electronic books must be kept on servers physically located in India on a periodic basis
Disclosure in Annual Report: company must disclose (in Board Report or notes to accounts) whether accounting software used has audit trail feature and whether it was operational throughout the year
rules.auditor_reporting_requirement: Auditors must comment on whether the audit trail feature was enabled and operated throughout the year in their audit report — per Companies (Audit and Auditors) Rules 2014 as amended
rules.amendment_ref: Companies (Accounts) Amendment Rules, 2021; Companies (Accounts) Fourth Amendment Rules, 2022

rules.title: Director Inspection Rights for Books of Account
rules.description: Books of account maintained in India must be open for inspection by any director of the company during business hours.
conditions: Any director may inspect books during business hours at the principal place or wherever books are maintained
To inspect books of a subsidiary company: only by a person specifically authorised by a Board resolution of the holding company
Officers and employees must render all reasonable assistance to the person inspecting
Improper refusal to allow inspection: punishable under Companies Act
